---
title: Syllabus
---

## Course description

This page documents the public syllabus for {{< meta course.title >}}.

Replace this text with a concise course-specific description after creating a repository from the template.

## Learning objectives

After the course, students should be able to:

- explain the central concepts introduced in the sessions;
- apply course methods to realistic tasks;
- discuss trade-offs and limitations;
- document their work reproducibly.

## Teaching approach

The template supports Git-based, reproducible, and contribution-oriented teaching.

Git sessions can draw on recommendations for teaching conceptual Git models before command memorization [@WagnerThurner2025].

Software-development courses can adapt the structured package-development course pattern and instructor-guide approach [@WagnerThurnerTangOtt2026].

Project-based open-source courses can use the CONTRIBUTE framework to scaffold student participation in authentic development projects [@ThurnerWagner2026].

## Assessment

Describe assessment rules in the instantiated course repository.

## Communication

Use stable course landing links instead of semester-specific LMS deep links where feasible.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
